rulings.Case Title: JOHN ROGERS VS. LYFT, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Dismissal With Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 18, 2026, line 1. PLAINTIFF JOHN ROGERS' Motion for Dismissal With Prejudice. Plaintiffs' motion to dismiss this case with prejudice is granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. The court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: AMIR GUPTA VS. LEAH CULVER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses From Pravana Capital Partners, Inc. To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For An Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 18, 2026, line 9. DEFENDANTS LEAH CULVER IN HER INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY AND IN HER CAPACITY AS TRUSTEE OF THE LEAH CULVER REVOCABLE TRUST'S Motion to Compel Further Responses From Pravana Capital Partners, Inc. To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For An Award Of Sanctions. Per the email dated March 12, 2026 from Kevin Rooney to the court, the only remaining issue on this motion is "whether Ms. Culver is entitled to receive bank records from Mr. Gupta and his entity (Pravana) involving other construction projects. The court resolves that issue as follows: No. Plaintiff Leah Culver has not shown that bank records unrelated to her construction project are directly relevant to any of the claims or defenses in this case. As financial records that implicate privacy concerns, bank records are typically discoverable only when they are directly relevant to a claim or defense rather than per the usual less stringent discovery standard. There is no reason to depart from the directly relevant standard for bank records in this case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions For Plaintiff'S Violation Of February 9, 2026 Court Order
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 18, 2026, line 4. DEFENDANT ATLASSIAN US, INC.'S Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions For Plaintiff's Violation Of February 9, 2026 Court Order. Defendant Atlassian US, Inc.'s motion for discovery sanctions is granted in part. Plaintiff failed to properly respond to defendant's set two requests for production of documents and defendant's set twos special interrogatories. No later than April 3, 2026 plaintiff shall serve verified further responses in compliance with this court's February 9, 2026 order. Plaintiff shall also pay $200 as reasonable sanctions to defendant no later than April 3, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: SARKIS GEGAMYAN VS. ATLASSIAN US, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 18, 2026, line 4. DEFENDANT ATLASSIAN US, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendant Atlassian US, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the first cause of action for disability discrimination, the fourth cause of action for retaliation and the sixth cause of action for wrongful termination and denied as to the second cause of action for failure to engage in the interactive process, the third cause of action for failure to provide reasonable accommodations, and the fifth cause of action for failure to prevent discrimination and retaliation. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM VILLARROEL ET AL VS. RECOLOGY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 18, 2026, line 6. DEFENDANTS GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY, RECOLOGY, INC., RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO, SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS. Defendants' motion to stay proceedings is denied and instead the court adopts the proposal of plaintiffs of "a partial stay, wherein discovery and motion practice may proceed but the case will not proceed to the summary motion phase or trial until the pending Federal appeal ... is complete." Defendants have not shown that permitting either discovery or motion practice to proceed would result in any unfairness, inefficiency or prejudice. Because a prior version of this case was filed five years ago, in the absence of any such unfairness, inefficiency or prejudice, it is eminently reasonable for discovery and motion practice to proceed forthwith. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RHOYALBAIBE DH FOSTON ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 18, 2026, line 7. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Amended MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to stay proceedings is granted. This case is stayed, including all discovery, until the resolution of the 36 related cases pending in the United States District Court for the Northern District of California or further order of this court. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
